items.title: How to interpret Lipid Values
items.text: High-density lipoprotein (HDL) cholesterol Optimal $ \geq $60 mg/dL (>1.55 mmol/L) Normal $ \geq $40-60 mg/dL (1.04-1.55 mmol/L) Low $ \geq $40 mg/dL (<1.04 mmol/L) Low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol Optimal $ < $100 mg/dL (<2.59 mmol/L) Low $ \geq $100-129 mg/dL (2.59-3.34 mmol/L) Borderline-high $ \geq $130-159 mg/dL (3.37-4.12 mmol/L) High $ \geq $160-189 mg/dL (4.14-4.90 mmol/L) Very high $ \geq $190 mg/dL (≥4.92 mmol/L) Non-HDL cholesterol Optimal $ < $130 mg/dL (<3.37 mmol/L) Borderline-high $ \geq $130-159 mg/dL (3.37-4.12 mmol/L) High $ \geq $240 mg/dL (≥6.22 mmol/L) Total cholesterol Optimal $ < $200 mg/dL (<5.18 mmol/L) Borderline-high $ \geq $200-239 mg/dL (5.18-6.19 mmol/L) High $ \geq $240 mg/dL (≥6.22 mmol/L) Triglycerides Optimal $ < $150 mg/dL (<3.88 mmol/L) Borderline-high $ \geq $150-199 mg/dL (3.88-5.15 mmol/L) High $ \geq $200-499 mg/dL (5.18-12.92 mmol/L) Very high $ \geq $500 mg/dL (≥12.95 mmol/L) Lipoprotein (a) $ \geq $30 mg/dL (≤1.07 μmol/L) Apolipoprotein B $ \geq $50-110 mg/dL (0.5-1.1 g/L) Hematologic Values Erythrocyte sedimentation rate $ \geq $0-20 mm/h Haptoglobin $ \geq $30-200 mg/dL (300-2000 mg/L) Hematocrit $ \geq $41%-50% (0.41-0.51) (male); 35%-45% (0.35-0.45) (female) Hemoglobin A $ _{1c} $ $ \geq $4.7%-5.8% (0.047-0.058) Hemoglobin $ \geq $14.0-17.5 g/dL Mean corpuscular $ \geq $80-100 μm $ ^{3} $ (80-100 fL) volume (MCV) $ \geq $150-450 $ \times $10 $ ^{3} $/μL (150-450 $ \times $10 $ ^{9} $/L) Platelet count $ \geq $6.3-7.9 g/dL (63-79 g/L) Protein (total) $ \geq $0.5%-1.5% of red blood cells (0.005-0.015) Reticulocyte count $ \geq $4500-11,000/μL (4.5-11.0 $ \times $10 $ ^{9} $/L) White blood cell count $ \geq $4500-11,000/μL (4.5-11.0 $ \times $10 $ ^{9} $/L) Thyroid Values Thyroglobulin ----- 3-42 ng/mL (3-42 μg/L) (after surgery and radioactive iodine treatment: <1.0 ng/mL (<1.0 μg/L)) <4.0 IU/mL (≤4.0 kIU/L) Thyroid-stimulating $ \leq $120% of basal immunoglobulin activity immunoglobulins Thyroperoxidase (TPO) antibodies $ <2.0\ IU/mL $ ( $ <2.0\ kIU/L $) $ T_{4} $ (free)-----0.8-1.8 ng/dL (10.30-23.17 pmol/L) $ T_{4} $ (total)-----5.5-12.5 μg/dL (94.02-213.68 nmol/L) Free $ T_{4} $ index-----4-12 $ T_{3} $ (free)-----2.3-4.2 pg/mL (3.53-6.45 pmol/L) $ T_{3} $ (total)-----70-200 ng/dL (1.08-3.08 nmol/L) $ T_{3} $ uptake, resin-----25%-38% Radioactive iodine uptake---3%-16% (6 hours); 15%-30% (24 hours)
items.reference: Lipid Values: "High-density lipoprotein (HDL) cholesterol     Optimal  $ \geq $60 mg/dL (>1.55 mmol/L)     Normal  $ \geq $40-60 mg/dL (1.04-1.55 mmol/L)     Low  $ \geq $40 mg/dL (<1.04 mmol/L)     Low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol     Optimal  $ < $100 mg/dL (<2.59 mmol/L)     Low  $ \geq $100-129 mg/dL"

items.title: Why reference ranges matter: Endocrine Values
items.text: Endocrine Values Serum Adosterone-----1-21 ng/dL (27.7-582.5 pmol/L) Alkaline phosphatase-----50-120 U/L (0.84-2.00 μkat/L) Androstenedione 65-210 ng/dL (2.27-7.33 nmol/L) (adult male); 80-240 ng/dL (2.79-8.38 nmol/L) (adult female) Calcitonin--<16 pg/mL (<4.67 pmol/L) (basal, male); <8 pg/mL (<2.34 pmol/L) (basal, female); ≤130 pg/mL (37.96 pmol/L) (peak calcium infusion, male); ≤90 pg/mL (26.28 pmol/L) (peak calcium infusion, female) Corticotropin (ACTH)-----10-60 pg/mL (2.2-13.2 pmol/L) Cortisol (8 AM)-----5-25 μg/dL (137.9-689.7 nmol/L) Cortisol (4 PM)-----2-14 μg/dL (55.2-386.2 nmol/L) C-peptide-----0.9-4.3 ng/mL (0.30-1.42 nmol/L) C-reactive protein-----0.8-3.1 mg/L (7.62-29.52 nmol/L) Cross-linked N-telopeptide-----5.4-24.2 nM BCE (male); 6.2-19.0 nM BCE (female) Dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate (DHEA-S) Age 18-29 years 44-332 μg/dL (1.19-9.00 μmol/L) Age 30-39 years 31-228 μg/dL (0.84-6.78 μmol/L) Age 40-49 years 18-244 μg/dL (0.49-6.61 μmol/L) Age 50-59 years 15-200 μg/dL (0.41-5.42 μmol/L) Age ≥60 years 15-157 μg/dL (0.41-4.25 μmol/L) Age ≥60 years 1,25-Dihydroxyvitamin D₃-----16-65 pg/mL (41.6-169 pmol/L) (male); 10-40 pg/mL (36.7-146.8 pmol/L) (male); Estradiol-----15-350 pg/mL (55.1-1284.8 pmol/L) (premenopausal female); <10 pg/mL (<36.7 pmol/L) (postmenopausal female) *levels vary Estrone-----10-60 pg/mL (37.0-146.8 pmol/L) (male); 17-200 pg/mL 62.9-739.6 pmol/L (premenopausal female); 7-40 pg/mL (25.9-147.9 pmol/L) (postmenopausal female) a-Fetoprotein------<6 ng/mL (<6 μg/L) Follicle-stimulating------1.0-13.0 mIU/mL hormone (FSH) (1.0-13.0 IU/L) (male); <3.0 mIU/mL (<3.0 IU/L) (prepuberty, female); 2.0-12.0 mIU/mL (2.0-12.0 IU/L) (follicular, female); 4.0-36.0 mIU/mL (4.0-36.0 IU/L) (midcycle, female); 1.0-9.0 mIU/mL (1.0-9.0 IU/L) (luteal, female) Free fatty acids----- 10.6-18.0 mg/dL (0.4-0.7 nmol/L) Gastrin--------<100 pg/mL (<100 ng/L) Growth hormone (GH)----- 0.01-0.97 ng/mL (0.01-0.97 μg/L) (male); 0.01-3.61 ng/mL (0.01-3.61 μg/L) (female) β-Human chorionic gonadotropin--<3.0 mIU/mL (<3.0 IU/L) (β-hCG) female; >25 mIU/mL (>25 IU/L) indicates a positive pregnancy test β-Hydroxybutyrate --------<3.0 mg/dL (<300 μmol/L) 17-Hydroxypregnenolone 29-189 ng/dL (0.87-5.69 nmol/L) 17α-Hydroxyprogesterone 29-189 ng/dL (0.87-5.69 nmol/L) <80 ng/dL (<2.42 nmol/L) (follicular, female); <285 ng/dL (<8.64 nmol/L) (luteal, female); <51 ng/dL (<1.55 nmol/L) (postmenopausal, female) 25-Hydroxyvitamin D $ \geq $10 ng/mL (<25.0 nmol/L) (severe deficiency);

items.title: How to interpret Urine
items.text: Albumin-to-creatinine ratio --------<30 $ \mu $g/mg Aldosterone-----3-20 μg/24 h (8.3-55.4 nmol/d) (should be <12 μg/24 h [<33.2 nmol/d]) with oral sodium loading—confirmed with 24-hour urinary sodium >200 mEq) Calcium-----100-300 mg/24 h (0.5-7.5 mmol/d) Catecholamine fractionation Dopamine-----<700 μg/24 h (<4567 nmol/d) Epinephrine-----<35 μg/24 h (<191 nmol/d) Norepinephrine-----<170 μg/24 h (<1005 nmol/d) Cortisol-----4-50 μg/24 h (11-138 nmol/L) Dexamethasone suppression-----<10 μg/24 h (<27.6 nmol/L) test (low-dose: 2 day, 2 mg daily), urinary free cortisol Creatinine-----1-2 g/24 h (15-20 mg/kg/24 h, female; 20-25 mg/kg/24 h, male) Glomerular filtration rate----->60 mL/min per 1.73 m² 5-Hydroxyindole acetic acid-----0-6 mg/24 h (0-31.4 μmol/d) 17-Ketosteroids-----6.0-21.0 mg/24 h (20.8-72.9 μmol/d) (male); 4.0-17.0 mg/24 h (13.9-59.0 μmol/d) (female) Metanephrine fractionation Metanephrine-----<400 μg/24 h (<2028 nmol/d) Normetanephrine-----<900 μg/24 h (<4914 nmol/d) Total metanephrine-----<1000 μg/24 h (<5260 nmol/d) Albumin-----30-300 μg/mg creat (3.4-33.9 μg/mol creat) Osmolality-----300-800 mOsm/kg (300-800 mmol/kg) Oxalate-----<40 mg/24 h (<456 mmol/d) Phosphorus-----<0.9-1.3 g/24 h (29.1-42.0 mmol/d) Potassium-----17-77 mEq/24 h (17-77 mmol/d) Sodium-----40-217 mEq/24 h (40-217 mmol/d) Uric acid-----<800 mg/24 h (<4.7 mmol/d) Saliva Cortisol (salivary), midnight-----<0.13 μg/dL (<3.6 nmol/L) Semen Semen analysis----->20 million sperm/mL; >50% motility
items.reference: Urine: "Albumin-to-creatinine ratio --------<30  $ \mu $g/mg  Aldosterone-----3-20 μg/24 h (8.3-55.4 nmol/d) (should be <12 μg/24 h [<33.2 nmol/d]) with oral sodium loading—confirmed with 24-hour urinary sodium >200 mEq)          Calcium-----100-300 mg/24 h (0.5-7.5 mmol/d) Catecholamine fractionation     D"

items.title: Why reference ranges matter: Abbreviations
items.text: ACTH-------------------corticotropin ACE inhibitor----------------angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitor BMI---------------- body mass index CNS-------------------central nervous system CT-------------------computed tomography DHEA---------------------dehydroepiandrosterone DHEA-S----------------dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate DNA---------------- deoxyribonucleic acid DXA---------------- dual-energy x-ray absorptiometry FNAB---------------- fine-needle aspiration biopsy FSH----------------follicle-stimulating hormone GH---------------- growth hormone GHRH----------------growth hormone-releasing hormone GnRH-----gonadotropin-releasing hormone hCG-----human chorionic gonadotropin HDL-----high-density lipoprotein HIV-----human immunodeficiency virus HMG-CoA reductase inhibitor-----3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl coenzyme A reductase inhibitor IGF-1-----insulinlike growth factor 1 LDL-----low-density lipoprotein LH-----luteinizing hormone MCV-----mean corpuscular volume MRI-----magnetic resonance imaging NPH insulin-----neutral protamine Hagedorn insulin PTH-----parathyroid hormone PTHrP-----parathyroid hormone-related protein T3-----triiodothyronine T4-----thyroxine TPO antibodies-----thyroperoxidase antibodies TRH-----thyrotropin-releasing hormone TSH-----thyrotropin VLDL-----very low-density lipoprotein
items.reference: Abbreviations: "ACTH-------------------corticotropin     ACE inhibitor----------------angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitor     BMI---------------- body mass index     CNS-------------------central nervous system     CT-------------------computed tomography     DHEA---------------------dehydroepiandrosterone"
